mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 220d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 220d engine typically costs £1,800–£4,000 supply only depending on generation, with B47 engines commanding a premium over N47 units. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Dealers charge £4,200–£5,200 for a new engine out of warranty. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which engine generation your 220d has — this badge spans the N47 (2014–2015, the well-documented rear-chain risk generation) and the B47 (2015–present, timing chain relocated to the front, meaningfully improved). The B47 is generally regarded as a strong and dependable engine, capable of high mileage if properly maintained. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 220d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (2014–2015) and early B47s (pre-2018), EGR cooler failure affecting both N47 and B47 engines (subject to global BMW recall covering 2013–2017 models), and DPF clogging on vehicles used for short urban journeys.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 220d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £7,000–£12,000 220d, a £2,500 recon is 20–35% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon approaches our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 220d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: pre-2015 is N47 (four-cylinder, rear-chain), 2015-onwards is B47 (four-cylinder, front-chain). F44 Gran Coupé uses B47C20B (transverse). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£1,800–£4,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. For B47 engines, BMW Service Bulletin SI B11 02 17 recommends inspection and replacement if rattle exceeds 3 seconds.

eraMap.title: BMW 220d — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: Four-cylinder, rear-mounted timing chain — well-documented failure risk
Four-cylinder, front-mounted chain — meaningfully improved over N47
FWD-based platform — same B47 family, transverse layout
Second-generation RWD coupé — B47 with 48V MHEV
Active/Gran Tourer — N47 in early cars, B47 from 2015

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 220d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The N47's rear-mounted chain makes timing-related labour more expensive. F44/G42 transverse layouts add complexity.

cta.label: → Compare 220d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N47 Rear Timing Chain Failure (F22/F23 pre-2015, F45 early)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle lasting 1–30 seconds, metallic ticking from the rear of the engine (against the firewall), engine warning light, loss of power

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: This is the defining issue of the N47 engine. The timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine, against the firewall, making access extremely difficult. The chain, guides and tensioners were poorly designed. BMW made improvements after 2015, so later models are generally more reliable. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately. A £650–£1,800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £1,800–£4,500 replacement that follows a snapped chain.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: B47 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (pre-2018)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle lasting 2–5 seconds, metallic noise from the front of the engine, P000A/P0016/P0017 fault codes, metal flakes in oil filter
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — less frequent than N47 but documented

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: BMW's B47 engine uses a single-row timing chain system prone to premature stretch and guide wear. BMW Service Bulletin SI B11 02 17 confirms timing chain tensioner wear as a known weakness in pre-2018 models, recommending inspection and replacement if rattle exceeds 3 seconds. BMW extended warranty coverage to 10 years / 120,000 miles for affected engines. If your car is now out of warranty, dealers charge £4,200–£5,200 for a new engine.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/b47-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (N47 and B47, all generations)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell, engine warning light, loss of power
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — subject to BMW recall
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: BMW recalled 1.6 million diesel vehicles globally over EGR cooler faults that could cause coolant leaks and, in rare cases, fire risk. The recall covers 2 Series models manufactured between July 2013 and December 2017. Glycol leakage from the EGR cooler can combine with soot deposits and lead to melting of the intake manifold. Verify recall completion before buying any 220d. Some owners report running issues after EGR replacement — ensure the work was completed correctly.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

problems.title: DPF Clogging (All Engines)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, loss of power, limp mode, poor throttle response, rising fuel consumption
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — short urban journeys accelerate the issue
problems.recommendation: Like all modern diesels, the 220d's DPF is sensitive to short urban journeys. The B47 has a reported service life of 210,000–360,000 km (130,000–220,000 miles) if properly maintained with correct quality oil and fuel. If you primarily drive short urban journeys, factor in regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regenerations. Some owners report DPF clogging requiring manual regeneration or cleaning.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF clogging guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 220d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 B47-engined car (timing chain relocated to the front — the cleaner slate)
* Documented timing chain history if buying an N47-engined car (pre-2015)
* Confirmed EGR recall completion — check DVSA recall R/2019/241
* Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
avoidIf: * N47-engined car (pre-2015) with cold-start rattle and no chain history — this is a ticking time bomb
* B47 pre-2018 with cold-start rattle lasting over 3 seconds
* No proof of EGR recall work
* Poor service history on any generation — diesel BMWs are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: Across the 220d's lifetime, the honest advice is simple: buy a B47 (2015-onwards) if you want the cleaner slate — the timing chain was relocated to the front of the engine, making it both more durable and easier to service. The N47's rear-mounted timing chain is a genuine weak point that BMW never fully resolved. However, early B47s (pre-2018) still have timing chain tensioner wear documented, and BMW's extended warranty program is worth checking. Buy on documented history, not on generation alone.

cta.label: → Compare 220d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 220d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning, N47) or 2–5 seconds (B47 pre-2018)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47 or B47) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* EGR Recall — Verify EGR cooler recall completion — DVSA recall R/2019/241
* DPF Health — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesels
* Turbo Health — Any whistling or loss of power? N47 turbo issues are documented
* Oil Consumption — N47s can consume >1.5L per 1,000km
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for P000A, P0016, P0017
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
